quizQuestion:
このまま放置すると、大事故に繋がり{___}。
(If left as is, it could lead to a serious accident.)
explanation: かねない (might/could — negative potential) — このまま放置すると、大事故に繋がりかねない。Warns of an undesirable possibility.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
あんな態度を取り続ければ、友人を失い{___}。
(If he keeps up that attitude, he might lose his friends.)
explanation: かねない (might/could) — あんな態度を取り続ければ、友人を失いかねない。Risk of losing something valuable.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
その薬を過剰に飲むと、体を害し{___}。
(Taking too much of that medicine could harm your body.)
explanation: かねない (could/might — warning) — その薬を過剰に飲むと、体を害しかねない。Medical warning context.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
このペースでは、締め切りに間に合わなく{___}。
(At this pace, we might not make the deadline.)
explanation: かねない (might/could) — このペースでは、締め切りに間に合わなくかねない。Deadline risk.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
先生に嘘をつこう{___}、大変なことになる。
(If you happen to lie to the teacher, it will become a serious matter.)
explanation: ようものなら (if one happens to/if by any chance) — 先生に嘘をつこうものなら、大変なことになる。Warns of severe consequences.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
失敗しよう{___}、全員に迷惑がかかる。
(If by chance we fail, it will cause trouble for everyone.)
explanation: ようものなら (if one happens to) — 失敗しようものなら、全員に迷惑がかかる。Conditional warning about shared consequences.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
秘密が漏れよう{___}、会社の信頼が失われる。
(If the secret were to leak, the company's credibility would be lost.)
explanation: ようものなら (if by chance/if it happens that) — 秘密が漏れようものなら、会社の信頼が失われる。Corporate risk scenario.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼女を怒らせよう{___}、後が怖い。
(If you happen to make her angry, the aftermath will be frightening.)
explanation: ようものなら (if one happens to) — 彼女を怒らせようものなら、後が怖い。Colloquial warning about provoking someone.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
そんなことは十分に起こり{___}。
(Such a thing could quite possibly happen.)
explanation: 得る (can/is possible) — そんなことは十分に起こり得る。Indicates logical possibility.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
これは絶対にあり{___}話だ。
(This is a story that absolutely cannot happen.)
explanation: 得ない (cannot/is impossible) — これは絶対にあり得ない話だ。Logical impossibility.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
最悪の場合、そのような事態も考え{___}。
(In the worst case, such a situation is also conceivable.)
explanation: 得る (can/possible) — 最悪の場合、そのような事態も考え得る。Formal possibility expression.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
努力すれば、誰でも成功し{___}。
(If one works hard, anyone can possibly succeed.)
explanation: 得る (can/possible) — 努力すれば、誰でも成功し得る。Universal possibility through effort.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
証拠がこんなにあるなら、認め{___}。
(With so much evidence, I have no choice but to admit it.)
explanation: ざるを得ない (have no choice but to) — 証拠がこんなにあるなら、認めざるを得ない。Reluctant necessity.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
台風が来るので、旅行をキャンセルせ{___}。
(Since a typhoon is coming, I have no choice but to cancel the trip.)
explanation: ざるを得ない (no choice but to) — 台風が来るので、旅行をキャンセルせざるを得ない。Force of nature requiring reluctant action.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
体調が悪いので、今日は休ま{___}。
(Since I feel unwell, I have no choice but to rest today.)
explanation: ざるを得ない (no choice but to) — 体調が悪いので、今日は休まざるを得ない。Health-related compulsion.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
会社の命令なので、従わ{___}。
(Since it is a company order, I have no choice but to comply.)
explanation: ざるを得ない (no choice but to) — 会社の命令なので、従わざるを得ない。Institutional authority forcing compliance.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
成功するかどうかは、本人の努力{___}。
(Whether one succeeds depends on the individual's effort.)
explanation: 次第だ/次第で (depends on) — 成功するかどうかは、本人の努力次第だ。Outcome is determined by a variable factor.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
天気{___}、ピクニックに行くかどうか決める。
(We will decide whether to go on a picnic depending on the weather.)
explanation: 次第で (depends on) — 天気次第で、ピクニックに行くかどうか決める。Decision contingent on an uncontrollable factor.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
やり方{___}、どんな仕事でも楽しくなる。
(Depending on the approach, any job can become enjoyable.)
explanation: 次第で (depending on) — やり方次第で、どんな仕事でも楽しくなる。Method as the variable.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
準備{___}、結果は大きく変わる。
(Depending on the preparation, the results will change significantly.)
explanation: 次第で (depending on) — 準備次第で、結果は大きく変わる。Preparation as the key variable.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
早めに準備する{___}。
(It is better to prepare early.)
explanation: に越したことはない (it is best to/better to) — 早めに準備するに越したことはない。Ideally something should be done a certain way.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
健康のためには、適度な運動をする{___}。
(For the sake of health, it is best to exercise moderately.)
explanation: に越したことはない (better to/best to) — 健康のためには、適度な運動をするに越したことはない。Health advice context.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
リスクを避ける{___}が、全て避けられるわけでもない。
(It is best to avoid risks, but not all can be avoided.)
explanation: に越したことはない (better to) — リスクを避けるに越したことはないが、全て避けられるわけでもない。Acknowledges ideal while noting limits.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
お金はあるに{___}が、なくても何とかなる。
(It is better to have money, but one can manage without it.)
explanation: に越したことはない (better to have/best if) — お金はあるに越したことはないが、なくても何とかなる。Pragmatic balance.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
もっと楽に仕事を終わらせる方法は{___}。
(If only there were an easier way to finish the work.)
explanation: ないものか (if only/I wish there were) — もっと楽に仕事を終わらせる方法はないものか。Expresses a wish or longing.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
この病気を治す薬が開発でき{___}。
(If only a cure for this disease could be developed.)
explanation: ないものか (if only) — この病気を治す薬が開発できないものか。Medical wish/longing.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
二人の仲を取り持つことはでき{___}と思っている。
(I am wondering if there is no way to mediate between the two.)
explanation: ないものか (if only/I wonder if there is a way) — 二人の仲を取り持つことはできないものかと思っている。Reflective wish.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
もう少しだけ給料を上げてもらえ{___}。
(If only I could get my salary raised a little more.)
explanation: ないものか (if only/I wish) — もう少しだけ給料を上げてもらえないものか。Common workplace wish.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
この証拠から、犯人は内部の人間である{___}。
(From this evidence, the culprit is thought to be an insider.)
explanation: ものと思われる (is thought to be) — この証拠から、犯人は内部の人間であるものと思われる。Formal inference based on evidence.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
このデータは正確である{___}。
(This data is thought to be accurate.)
explanation: ものと思われる (is thought to be) — このデータは正確であるものと思われる。Formal presumption in written/official contexts.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼は既に海外に逃亡した{___}。
(He is thought to have already fled abroad.)
explanation: ものと思われる (is thought to have/be) — 彼は既に海外に逃亡したものと思われる。Police or investigative report style.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
この技術は将来広く普及する{___}。
(This technology is thought to spread widely in the future.)
explanation: ものと思われる (is thought to be) — この技術は将来広く普及するものと思われる。Forward-looking formal prediction.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
この薬は副作用が少ない{___}。
(This medicine is said to have few side effects.)
explanation: とされる (is said to be/is considered to be) — この薬は副作用が少ないとされる。Passive reporting of common belief or claim.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
この遺跡は約2000年前のもの{___}。
(These ruins are said to be from about 2000 years ago.)
explanation: とされる (is said to be) — この遺跡は約2000年前のものとされる。Historical or academic claim.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼は業界で最も優秀な技術者{___}。
(He is considered to be the most skilled engineer in the industry.)
explanation: とされる (is considered to be) — 彼は業界で最も優秀な技術者とされる。Recognition by industry consensus.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
睡眠は健康に不可欠{___}。
(Sleep is considered to be indispensable for health.)
explanation: とされる (is considered to be) — 睡眠は健康に不可欠とされる。Widely held belief stated formally.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
